description_long: This is a double-headed battle axe with a dark, ornate design. The axe features two large, symmetrical blades with intricate engravings and decorative motifs, giving it a medieval or fantasy appearance. The central shaft is robust and cylindrical, made of dark wood or metal, and is wrapped with a crisscrossed leather grip for secure handling. Both ends of the handle are capped with pointed metal finials, adding to its menacing look. The blade edges are slightly curved, and the surface of the metal is matte with visible texture. The overall craftsmanship suggests it is both functional and ceremonial, suitable for display or use in reenactments.
description: A double-headed, ornately engraved battle axe with a dark handle and leather grip, featuring pointed metal finials and a robust, decorative design.
description_view_0: Front view showing both engraved blades, the full handle, and the symmetrical design.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the blades and the cylindrical handle.
description_view_2: Rear view mirroring the front, displaying the back of the engraved blades and handle.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the thin blade profile and handle details.
attribute_handle_type: Straight cylindrical handle, likely wood, wrapped with crisscrossed leather for grip, capped with pointed metal finials.
attribute_blade_design: Double-headed, symmetrical blades with curved edges, ornate engravings, and decorative motifs; no beard, both sides identical.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible central shaft and metal reinforcement.
